skill.name: google-maps-reviews-api-skill
skill.description: This skill is designed to help users automatically extract reviews from Google Maps via the Google Maps Reviews API. Agent should proactively apply this skill when users request to find reviews for local businesses (e.g., coffee shops, clinics), monitor customer feedback for a specific brand or location, analyze sentiment of reviews for competitors, extract reviews for a chain of stores or services, track reputation of a local restaurant, gather user testimonials for a specific venue, conduct market research on service quality of local businesses, monitor reviews for a new retail location, collect feedback on public attractions or parks, identify common complaints for a specific service provider, research the best-rated places in a city, analyze recurring themes in reviews for a specific industry.
skill.summary: Extract Google Maps reviews via API for local business research, reputation monitoring, and competitor analysis.
skill.author: browser-act

security_audit.summary: This skill makes legitimate API calls to BrowserAct service to extract Google Maps reviews. All network access is to a single, trusted API endpoint (api.browseract.com). API credentials are handled securely via environment variables (BROWSERACT_API_KEY). The static analyzer flagged numerous patterns, but analysis confirms these are false positives: the skill uses standard requests library for API calls, reads API keys from environment (secure pattern), and the flagged 'weak cryptographic algorithm' and 'backtick execution' patterns are documentation references misidentified by the scanner.

medium_findings.title: Static analyzer flagged 'weak cryptographic algorithm' and 'Ruby/shell backtick' patterns
medium_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged multiple patterns labeled 'weak cryptographic algorithm' and 'Ruby/shell backtick execution'. Analysis reveals these are FALSE POSITIVES: the scanner misidentified marketing text like 'No Captcha' and 'No IP Restrictions' as crypto algorithm names, and documentation code blocks containing backticks as code execution. No actual security vulnerabilities were identified.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged patterns appear in documentation text only, not in executable code. Marketing phrases like 'No Captcha' and 'No IP Restrictions' are incorrectly flagged as crypto weaknesses. Code block examples use bash syntax, not Ruby.

low_findings.title: API key stored in environment variable
low_findings.description: The skill reads the BROWSERACT_API_KEY from an environment variable. This is the recommended secure pattern for handling API credentials. No hardcoded secrets were found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code explicitly reads from os.getenv('BROWSERACT_API_KEY') - this is the secure credential management pattern, not a vulnerability.

low_findings.title: Network requests to external API
low_findings.description: The skill makes HTTP requests to api.browseract.com. This is expected behavior for an API integration skill. No data exfiltration patterns were detected.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All network requests go to the legitimate BrowserAct API endpoint. No suspicious domains or exfiltration patterns detected.

content.user_title: Extract Google Maps Reviews via API
content.value_statement: Manually collecting customer reviews from Google Maps is time-consuming and inconsistent. This skill automates review extraction through a reliable API, providing structured data for reputation analysis, competitive research, and customer insights.
actual_capabilities: Extract structured review data (author, rating, text, date) from Google Maps search results
Filter reviews by language and country/region parameters
Poll async task status with real-time progress monitoring
Return clean, structured JSON output for downstream processing
Handle API errors gracefully with user-friendly error messages
Authenticate securely via environment variable (no hardcoded credentials)
limitations: Requires a BrowserAct API key to function
Does not provide historical review data beyond recent reviews
Rate limits depend on BrowserAct API plan
Cannot extract reviews from private or restricted Google Maps listings

use_cases.title: Local Business Reputation Analysis
use_cases.description: Research customer feedback for restaurants, clinics, or shops in a specific area. Use the structured review data to identify service strengths, common complaints, and rating trends.

use_cases.title: Competitive Benchmarking
use_cases.description: Extract and compare reviews from multiple competitors to understand market positioning. Identify what customers value most in your industry.

use_cases.title: Lead Qualification
use_cases.description: Gather review data to identify high-quality service providers or partners. Analyze ratings and feedback patterns to qualify leads.
use_cases.target_user: Sales teams, procurement specialists, business development

prompt_templates.title: Extract reviews for a specific business
prompt_templates.prompt: Find Google Maps reviews for [business name or type] in [location]. Extract the reviews with ratings and dates.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need structured review data for a specific business or category

prompt_templates.title: Compare competitor reviews
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare the customer reviews for [competitor A] versus [competitor B] in [city]. List the main strengths and weaknesses from each.

prompt_templates.title: Monitor reputation over time
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract recent reviews for [business name] locations in [region]. Identify any recurring complaints or emerging issues.
prompt_templates.scenario: When tracking brand reputation or service quality

prompt_templates.title: Research best-rated options
prompt_templates.prompt: Find the highest-rated [business type] in [neighborhood or city]. Get their review ratings and key feedback points.
prompt_templates.scenario: When helping users make informed decisions

output_examples.input: Find reviews for coffee shops in downtown Seattle
output_examples.output: Extracted 15 reviews from top-rated coffee shops in downtown Seattle. Average rating: 4.2 stars. Key themes: great espresso quality (mentioned 8 times), fast service (6 mentions), limited seating (4 complaints).

Compared customer reviews for 5 dentists in Manhattan. Average rating: 4.5 stars. Key differentiators: gentle approach (mentioned 12 times), modern equipment (9 mentions), wait times vary by location (2-45 minutes).

best_practices: Always provide specific search keywords and location for accurate results
Set the language and country parameters to match your target region
Review the API error messages to diagnose authentication or connectivity issues
anti_patterns: Do not use vague keywords like 'good restaurant' without location context
Do not ignore API key configuration errors; the skill requires valid BrowserAct credentials
Do not request reviews from restricted or private Google Maps listings

faq.question: What do I need to use this skill?
faq.answer: You need a BrowserAct API key. Set it as the BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable or provide it when prompted.

faq.question: How long does it take to get results?
faq.answer: The task typically takes 1-3 minutes depending on the number of reviews requested. The script outputs real-time status updates while polling.

faq.question: Can I extract reviews from any business?
faq.answer: The skill works with publicly available Google Maps listings. Reviews from private or restricted profiles cannot be extracted.

faq.question: How many reviews can I get per request?
faq.answer: The number of reviews depends on your BrowserAct API plan limits and the available reviews for the search query.

faq.question: What languages are supported?
faq.answer: You can specify language codes like 'en', 'zh-CN', 'es', 'fr', and many others. Results will be in the specified language.

faq.question: What should I do if the task fails?
faq.answer: Check the error output. If it shows 'Invalid authorization', verify your API key. For other errors, the skill will automatically retry once before reporting the failure.
